title: How do I create a Taskorilla account?
content: Click 'Sign Up,' enter your email, pick a password, choose between Helper, Tasker, or both, and confirm. You're ready to post or do tasks.

title: Do I need a verified ID to start?
content: ID verification is not required to start and is currently coming soon. Once available, verification will help build trust with other users and unlock higher-value tasks.

title: Can I use Taskorilla on mobile?
content: Yep! Taskorilla works on mobile, just open it in your browser and add it to your home screen like an app.

title: How do I edit my profile?
content: Go to 'My Profile,' update your info, skills, or profile picture, and hit save.

title: Is Taskorilla the same as Task Gorilla?
content: Yes. Many people search for Task Gorilla or Taskgorilla, but the correct name is Taskorilla. It's the place to post a task and have local helpers come to you.

title: How do I post a task?
content: Click 'Post a Task,' describe what you need, set location, time, and budget. Then hit 'Post.'

title: Can I edit a task after posting?
content: Yes, you can edit the description, budget, or deadline before anyone accepts it.

title: Can I cancel a task?
content: Yes. If you need to cancel, message the other party first through the task conversation. If payment has already been made, contact support for the next steps.

title: How do I find Helpers for my task?
content:
Once posted, Helpers in your area get a notification. You can also browse profiles and invite them directly.

When the Helper Task Match feature is enabled, suitable Helpers may also receive "New task" emails based on their skills and distance settings. This helps your task reach people who are genuinely a good fit.

title: What is the double handshake?
content: Both the Helper and Tasker must confirm a task before it's official. It prevents misunderstandings and ensures everyone is ready to commit.

title: How does bidding work with double handshake?
content: Helpers bid on a task, Taskers select a bid, and the Helper confirms. Only after both steps is the task locked in.

title: Why does Taskorilla use double handshake?
content: To make the platform fair, clear, and safe. Both sides commit only when ready, which builds trust.

title: How do I become a Helper?
content: Sign up, complete your profile, verify your ID, and you're ready to browse tasks.

title: Can I accept multiple tasks at once?
content: Yes, but only take what you can realistically complete on time.

title: How do I mark a task as completed?
content: Open the task in your dashboard and click 'Mark as Completed.' The poster will then confirm.

title: What if I can't finish a task?
content: Communicate with the Tasker immediately. Unfinished tasks can affect your rating.

title: How do I accept a task?
content: Taskorilla uses a double handshake. First, submit your bid. Then the Tasker selects your bid. Finally, you confirm the task. Only after both sides confirm is the task officially agreed. Start work only after payment is confirmed.

title: How should I price my bid?
content: Always enter your full price in euros (including time, materials, transport, and fees) and use the Price Justification box to explain what the Tasker gets for that amount. Once the Tasker selects your bid and you confirm the task (double handshake), the agreed price is locked in, so only commit to a number you are happy to honour.

title: How do "New task near you" emails work?
content:
Taskorilla can email you when a new task looks like a good match for you.

We decide which helpers to notify based on your skills and professions (we match the task’s tags and required skills to the skills and professions listed in your profile), and on location and distance (we use the task’s location to calculate distance). We also take into account your Preferred max distance for task matches (km) setting in your Helper Profile. The default is 100 km, and if you leave it blank we treat it as 100 km.

You will receive at most one "New task near you" email per task. Your email frequency setting controls when that email is sent: Instant (as soon as the task is posted and matched), Daily summary (grouped into a daily email), or Weekly summary (grouped into a weekly email). You can change both your preferred distance and email frequency anytime in My Profile → Helper Profile.

title: How does payment work?
content: Taskers pay through our secure system after selecting a bid and receiving helper confirmation (double handshake). Helpers get paid after the task is confirmed as complete.

title: Are there fees?
content: Yes. As of December 2025, Taskorilla charges the tasker a €2 service fee and charges helpers a 10% commission.

title: How do I get paid?
content: Add your PayPal email. Once a task is confirmed, we transfer funds automatically. Don't have a PayPal account yet? Create one [here](https://www.paypal.com/signup) and get set up in minutes.

title: How do I stay safe when meeting Helpers or Taskers?
content: Meet in public spaces when possible, verify profiles, and never share unnecessary personal info.

title: Can I block a Helper or Tasker?
content: Yes. Go to their profile and click 'Block User.' They won't see or message you.

title: What if something gets damaged during a task?
content: Always document it with photos and contact the other party.

title: Can I delete my Taskorilla account?
content: Yes, but note that completed task history may remain for record-keeping.

title: Why can't I post a task or accept one?
content: Check if your account is verified and that you haven't been suspended for any reason.

title: How do I reset my password?
content: Click 'Forgot Password,' enter your email, and follow the link we send.

title: Why am I not seeing certain tasks?
content:
Tasks are shown based on location, skills, and availability. Update your skills, services, and location in your profile for better matches.

If you’re a Helper, also check your Preferred max distance for task matches (km) and Email frequency for new task matches in your Helper Profile. These settings control which tasks are considered "near you" and how often you receive "New task near you" emails. If your distance is set very low, or your email frequency is set to weekly, you may see fewer notifications.

title: How do I contact support?
content: Use the Contact Support link in the Help Center to email us at tee@taskorilla.com. We usually reply within 24 hours.

title: Getting Started with Taskorilla
content:
**Step 1: Sign Up**

Click 'Sign Up,' enter your email, create a password, and confirm. You'll receive a confirmation email, click the link to verify your account.

**Step 2: Verify Your Account (Coming Soon)**

Verifying your ID helps build trust with other users and unlocks higher-value tasks. Go to 'My Profile' and upload a photo ID.

**Step 3: Set Up Your Profile**

Add a profile picture, list your skills (if you plan to be a Helper), and write a short bio. A complete profile makes you more trustworthy and helps you get matched with the right tasks.

**Step 4: Browse Tasks or Post a Task**

Decide whether you want to post a task (if you need help) or browse tasks (if you want to earn money as a Helper). Click 'Post a Task' or 'Browse Tasks' from the main menu.

**Step 5: Set Your Task Match Preferences (Helpers)**

In your Helper Profile, you can set how far you're willing to travel (Preferred max distance for task matches (km), default 100 km) and how often you receive "New task near you" emails (instant, daily, or weekly). This helps us notify you only about tasks that are truly relevant to you.

**Step 6: Check Notifications**

Keep track of messages, bids, and task updates through your dashboard and email notifications. Stay responsive to build a good reputation!

title: Quick Start for Taskers
content:
**Step 1: Create Your Account**

Sign up, verify your email, and complete your profile basics.

**Step 2: Post a Task**

Click 'Post New Task' and add a clear title, description, location, and due date.

**Step 3: Review and Refine Bids**

You may receive many bids. Compare prices, profiles, ratings, and messages.

Use the chat to go back and forth with helpers to clarify scope, timing, and what is included in the price until both sides fully understand the task.

**Step 4: Complete Double Handshake**

When you are happy, select the bid you want. The helper must then confirm the task. Only after both confirmations is the task officially agreed.

**Step 5: Pay Securely**

Complete payment in Taskorilla. The helper is only told to commence work after payment is confirmed.

**Step 6: Complete and Review**

When the work is done, confirm completion and leave a review.

title: How to Post a Task
content:
**Step 1: Navigate to Post a Task**

Click the 'Post New Task' button from the navigation bar or the Browse Tasks page. You'll be taken to the task creation form.

**Step 2: Choose Your Form Type**

You'll see two options at the top:

• Quick Form — A streamlined questionnaire-style form, great for simple tasks. Simply answer the questions and you'll be guided through the process automatically.

• Full Form — The complete form with all options, best for detailed tasks. The steps below describe the Full Form.

**Step 3: Select the Type of Help You Need**

Choose one of:

• Hire a Helper — For general tasks like cleaning, moving, errands, or handyman work.

• Engage a Professional — For specialist work like legal advice, accounting, therapy, or marketing.

**Step 4: Fill In the Task Details**

• Task Title (required) — A short, clear name, e.g. 'Need help moving furniture'.

• Description (required) — A detailed explanation of what needs to be done.

• Task Images (optional) — Upload photos to help helpers understand the task.

• Budget (optional) — Your budget in euros. Leave blank and helpers will submit quotes.

• Category (optional) — Pick the closest category, e.g. Home & Garden, Business.

• Sub-Category (optional) — Appears after selecting a category for more precision.

• Tags (optional) — Keywords to help your task get found. Press Enter after each tag.

• Required Skills (optional) — Specific skills needed. Press Enter after each.

• Professional Roles (optional) — Only for Professional tasks. Select roles like Accountant, Lawyer, etc.

**Step 5: Set Your Location**

• Country (required) — Select from the dropdown. This is auto-filled from your profile.

• Postcode — Enter your postcode and the system will auto-detect your location.

• Location — Your neighbourhood or city name. This is auto-filled from the postcode, or you can type it manually.

**Step 6: Set a Due Date**

Choose when you need the task completed. This defaults to today's date.

**Step 7: Post Your Task (Updated)**

Click 'Post Task'. If you're not logged in, you'll be prompted to log in first — your draft is automatically saved so you won't lose any details.

**Double Handshake Bidding:**

Once your task is live, helpers in your area can submit bids with their price and a message.

Review the bids and select the Helper you want.

The Helper must confirm the task. Only after this second handshake is the task officially agreed.

This ensures both sides explicitly agree before work starts, reducing misunderstandings and building trust.

**What Happens Next? (Updated)**

1. Your task goes live and appears in Browse Tasks for helpers to see.

2. Helpers submit bids with their price and a message.

3. You'll be notified when bids come in.

4. Double Handshake: Review bids, check helper profiles and ratings, then select a bid. The Helper must confirm — only after this is the task officially accepted.

5. Complete payment — the helper is only told to start work after payment is confirmed.

6. After payment, the contact filter is lifted in your conversation — you can now share phone numbers, email, WhatsApp, or other contact details directly with the helper. Before payment, contact information is blocked in messages for everyone's safety.

7. Once the helper completes the work, you can rate and review them.

**Requesting a Specific Helper (One-to-One) (Optional Update)**

If you've found a helper you like from the Helpers or Professionals page:

1. Go to their profile and click 'Request This Helper'.

2. The form will pre-fill with their name and offerings. Pick a skill or offering from their list, or type your own. Fill in the remaining details and post.

3. Double Handshake applies here too: The helper must confirm your request before the task is officially agreed.

title: How to Request a Specific Helper (One-to-One)
content:
**What is a One-to-One Request?**

A one-to-one request lets you send a task directly to a specific helper or professional. Only you and that helper can see the task — it's private and not visible to other users or the public.

**Step 1: Find the Helper You Want**

Browse the Helpers or Professionals page, or search for someone by name, skill, or service. You can also discover helpers through ratings, reviews, or featured profiles.

**Step 2: Visit Their Profile**

Click on the helper's name or avatar to open their full profile page. You'll see their skills, services offered, ratings, reviews, hourly rate, and professional offerings.

**Step 3: Click 'Request This Helper' (or 'Send Request')**

On their profile, click the request button. If the helper has listed specific offerings or skills, you can also click directly on one of those — this pre-selects that offering for your request.

**Step 4: The Task Form Opens Pre-Filled**

You'll be taken to the task creation form with:

• The helper's name and avatar shown at the top in a highlighted box.

• A 'Focus of this request' field — pre-filled with the offering you clicked, or type your own.

• The task title auto-generated (e.g. 'Branding with Antonio').

• The description auto-generated with a personalised message asking about availability and pricing.

• If the helper has multiple offerings, you can click different skill/offering pills to switch — the title and description update automatically.

**Step 5: Fill In the Remaining Details**

• Adjust the title and description if needed.

• Set your country and postcode (auto-filled from your profile).

• Set a due date.

• Optionally set a budget — or leave it blank to receive a quote from the helper.

Note: Images, categories, tags, and skills fields are hidden for one-to-one requests since the task is tailored to a specific person.

**Step 6: Post the Task**

Click 'Post Task'. The following happens:

1. The task is created as a private task assigned to that helper.

2. A conversation is automatically created between you and the helper.

3. A message is sent in that conversation with a link to the task.

4. The helper receives an email notification about the new request.

**Step 7: Wait for Their Quote**

If you left the budget blank, you'll see a message on the task page: 'Waiting for [helper name] to submit a quote.' The helper will submit a bid with their price and message.

**Step 8: Complete Double Handshake and Pay**

Once the helper submits their quote, review it and select the bid you want.

The helper must then confirm the task. Only after this second confirmation is the double handshake complete and the task officially agreed.

Payment is required before the helper is told to start work.

**Step 9: Communicate Freely After Payment**

Once payment is confirmed, the contact information filter is lifted in your conversation with the helper. You can now share phone numbers, email addresses, WhatsApp details, or any other contact information directly in the chat. This is especially useful for:

• Booking a first session with a therapist or coach.

• Coordinating a large contract where ongoing communication is needed.

• Paying a deposit for a bigger project and then arranging the details directly.

Before payment, contact details are blocked for everyone's safety. After payment, you're free to communicate however works best for you both.

**Optional: Open to All Helpers**

If the helper doesn't respond or you change your mind, you can click 'Open to all helpers' on the task page. This removes the assignment and makes the task visible to everyone. You'll be warned that the task title and description may need editing since they were written for a specific person.

**Optional: Remove the Helper**

While creating the task, you can click 'Remove helper' at any time to clear the assignment and switch to a normal public task.

title: How to Accept a One-to-One Offer
content:
**Step 1: You Receive a Notification**

When someone sends you a one-to-one request, you'll receive:

• An email notification with details about the new task request.

• A new message in your Conversations with a link to the task.

• The task will appear in your 'My Tasks' filter on the Browse Tasks page.

**Step 2: Review the Task**

Click the link from the email or message to view the full task details. You'll see:

• What the tasker needs (title and description).

• Their location and due date.

• Whether they've set a budget or are requesting a quote from you.

**Step 3: Submit Your Bid (Quote)**

If the tasker left the budget blank, you'll see a prompt: 'Submit your quote (amount and message) so the tasker can select your bid, you can confirm, and payment can proceed.'

Scroll down to the Bids section and:

Enter your price — the amount you'd charge for the task.

Write a message — confirm your availability, mention any preparation needed, and provide any relevant details.

Click 'Submit Bid'.

If the tasker already set a budget, you can still submit a bid with your own price and message.

Note: Submitting a bid does not officially confirm the task. The tasker must select your bid, and you must confirm the task — this is the double handshake.

**Step 4: Wait for Acceptance and Payment**

The tasker reviews your quote.

If they accept your bid, confirm the task when prompted so the double handshake is complete.

You'll receive an email: 'Your bid was selected — awaiting payment confirmation.'

Do not start work yet. Wait for bid selection, your confirmation, and payment confirmation.

**Step 5: Payment Confirmed — Start Work**

The task moves to 'In Progress' status.

You receive a second email: 'Payment confirmed — you can start!'

Now the task is officially confirmed — the double handshake is complete.

You can begin working on the task.

The contact information filter is lifted — you and the tasker can now share phone numbers, email, WhatsApp, or any other contact details directly in the conversation.

**Step 6: Track Progress and Complete**

• Add progress updates on the task page to keep the tasker informed.

• Upload progress photos if relevant.

• When finished, click 'Mark as Complete'.

• Upload a completion photo if prompted.

**Step 7: Get Paid and Reviewed**

Once the tasker confirms completion:

Payment is released to your PayPal account (minus the platform fee).

The tasker can leave a review and rating.

Once the tasker has reviewed you, you can review them back.

**What If You Don’t Want the Task?**

You're not obligated to accept. You can simply:

• Not submit a bid — the tasker will see no response.

• Message the tasker through the conversation to explain or negotiate.

• After 48 hours with no activity, the tasker may choose to open the task to all helpers.

title: Working as a Helper
content:
**Step 1: Complete Your Helper Profile**

After signing up, complete your Helper profile. Add your skills, experience, availability, hourly rate, and any tools/equipment you have. Upload a profile picture, it builds trust.

**Step 2: Browse Available Tasks**

Go to 'Browse Tasks' to see tasks near you that match your skills. Use filters to narrow down by category, distance, or budget.

**Step 3: Submit a Bid**

Found a task you can do? Click 'Submit Bid,' explain why you're a good fit, and confirm your availability. Be clear and professional.

**Step 4: Double Handshake Confirmation**

After you submit a bid, the tasker reviews bids and may select yours. The task is not officially agreed yet.

You must confirm the task after your bid is selected. Only when both sides have confirmed is the double handshake complete.

Do not start work yet.

**Step 5: Wait for Payment Confirmation**

Once the double handshake is complete, the task moves to payment.

Start work only after payment is confirmed and the task moves to 'In Progress'.

Important: Before payment is confirmed, contact information (phone numbers, email addresses, etc.) is blocked in messages for everyone's safety. Once payment is confirmed, the filter is lifted and you can freely share contact details in the conversation.

**Step 6: Complete the Task**

Show up on time, do the job well, and follow the tasker's instructions. Add progress updates or photos when needed to keep everything clear.

When finished, click 'Mark as Completed' in your dashboard.

**Step 7: Get Paid and Build Your Reputation**

After the tasker confirms completion, payment is released to your payout method.

Ask for reviews after every task. A high rating and positive feedback mean more jobs and better pay.

title: Quick Start for Helpers
content:
**Step 1: Complete Your Helper Profile**

Add your skills, services, availability, and profile photo so taskers can trust your profile.

**Step 2: Browse Tasks**

Use filters for category, distance, and budget to find relevant tasks.

**Step 3: Submit and Refine Your Bid**

Many helpers may bid on the same task. Send your full price and a short professional message explaining your availability and approach.

Be ready to go back and forth with the tasker in chat to clarify details, adjust scope, and update your bid until both sides are happy that the task is fully understood.

**Step 4: Complete Double Handshake**

If the tasker selects your bid, confirm the task when prompted. Only then is the task officially agreed.

**Step 5: Wait for Payment Confirmation**

Do not commence work until payment is confirmed and the task moves to 'In Progress'.

**Step 6: Finish Strong**

Add progress updates if needed, mark the task complete, and ask for a review to build your reputation.

title: Understanding Payments on Taskorilla
content:
**Step 1: Add Your Payout Method**

Head to Profile > Add your PayPal email. This lets you receive payments easily as a Helper or Professional. Don't have a PayPal account yet? Create one here and get set up in minutes.

**Step 2: Understand Fees**

Taskorilla takes a small service fee from each transaction. As of December 2025, fees are €2 per task from the tasker and 10% fee from the Helper/Professional.

**Step 3: Make or Receive Payment**

**For Taskers:** After you select a bid and the helper confirms the task (double handshake), you'll be prompted to pay securely through our platform. Funds are held until the task is confirmed as complete.

**For Helpers:** Once a task is marked complete and confirmed by the tasker, payment is released to your account automatically.

**Step 4: Track Your Payments and Payouts**

Check your dashboard for payment history, pending payments or payouts, and completed transactions. You'll receive email confirmations for every payment or payout.

**Step 5: Payouts (for Helpers and Professionals)**

Payments are transferred to your linked account automatically after task confirmation.

title: Staying Safe on Taskorilla
content:
**Step 1: Verify Profiles Before Working Together**

Always check users' profiles, reviews, and verification status before accepting or assigning a task. Verified users with good reviews are more trustworthy.

**Step 2: Meet in Public Spaces When Possible**

For tasks that don't require being at someone's home (like delivery or moving), arrange to meet in a public location first.

**Step 3: Communicate Clearly Through the Platform**

Use Taskorilla's messaging system for all communication. This keeps a record and helps resolve disputes. Never share personal contact info like phone numbers or emails early on.

**Step 4: Protect Your Personal Information**

Don't overshare. Your profile should include enough to build trust, but not sensitive details like your full address, bank info, or personal identification numbers.

**Step 5: Document Everything**

Take photos before and after completing a task. This protects both parties in case of damage claims or disputes about work quality.

**Step 6: Trust Your Instincts**

If something feels off, it probably is. Don't proceed with a task if you're uncomfortable. You can always cancel and report suspicious behavior.

**Step 7: Report Problems Immediately**

If someone is harassing you, acting suspiciously, or breaking platform rules, use the 'Report' button on their profile. We take safety seriously and will investigate promptly.

**Step 8: Review Safety Tips Regularly**

Check our Help Center for updated safety guidelines. Stay informed about common scams and how to avoid them.

title: Marketplace Safety Guidelines
content:
**Last Updated: February 26th, 2026**

Taskorilla does not verify every user, supervise tasks, or guarantee that every experience will be safe. Always use caution.

**Safety Tips**

- Meet in public places whenever possible.

- Avoid tasks that feel risky or unsafe.

- Trust your instincts and walk away if something feels off.

- Before payment is confirmed, Taskorilla blocks sharing contact details and off-platform references in messages. After payment confirmation, this restriction is lifted in task conversations.

- Report suspicious behaviour immediately within the platform.

**Platform Restrictions**

Taskorilla does not allow illegal tasks, dangerous activities, offensive behaviour, or attempts to move conversations off the platform to avoid our rules.

**Emergency Situations**

If you ever feel unsafe, contact local authorities right away. Taskorilla is not an emergency service.

title: Disputes and Refunds
content:
**Last Updated: February 26th 2026**

Taskorilla is not party to agreements between Taskers and Helpers. Any disputes must be handled directly between those two users.

Taskorilla does not provide refunds for task payments unless there is a confirmed platform error, such as a duplicated charge caused by the platform itself.

Taskorilla does not mediate disagreements, guarantee outcomes, or compensate users for incomplete tasks, dissatisfaction, or disagreements between Taskers and Helpers.

title: GDPR Data Processing Add-On
content:
**Last Updated: November 17, 2025**

This add-on applies to users in the European Economic Area (EEA) and the United Kingdom.

**Data Controller**

Taskorilla is the data controller for the information you provide through the platform.

**Your Rights Under GDPR**

You may request access, correction, deletion, restriction of processing, data portability, or withdrawal of consent. Contact us to exercise these rights.

**Data Transfers**

We may transfer data outside the EEA or UK using legally recognised safeguards such as Standard Contractual Clauses.

**Obligations**

Do not upload information about others unless you have their permission.

title: Handyman Prices in Portugal (2026 Guide)
content:
Wondering how much a handyman costs in Portugal? Prices vary depending on the job, location, and experience of the helper. This guide gives you a realistic idea of what you might pay for common home tasks in the Algarve and across Portugal.

**1. Handyman / Plumbing**

| Task | Typical Price |
| --- | --- |
| Fix leaking tap | €40 – €90 |
| Replace bathroom faucet | €50 – €120 |
| Unclog sink / drain | €40 – €90 |
| Fix running toilet | €50 – €110 |
| Install shower head / hose | €40 – €90 |
| Replace shower valves | €80 – €160 |
| Fix water heater / boiler leak | €80 – €200 |
| Repair or replace sink | €60 – €150 |
| Install washing machine / dishwasher | €50 – €120 |
| Repair garden tap / outdoor faucet | €40 – €90 |
| Replace bathroom sink plug | €30 – €70 |
| Install new shower curtain rod | €30 – €60 |
| Replace toilet seat | €20 – €50 |
| Replace tap washers / cartridges | €25 – €60 |
| Fix leaking radiator | €50 – €120 |
| Install bathroom accessories (towel rail, toilet roll holder) | €30 – €80 |
| Install or replace water filter | €40 – €90 |
| Repair door lock | €40 – €90 |
| Replace broken tiles | €50 – €150 |
| Minor pipe repair (visible pipes) | €50 – €120 |
| Seal bathroom corners / silicone | €40 – €80 |
| Fix shower door / hinge | €50 – €120 |
| Replace bathroom mirror | €30 – €80 |
| Repair toilet flush mechanism | €40 – €90 |
| Install small sink cabinet | €50 – €120 |
| Install hot water booster / inline heater | €60 – €150 |
| Replace toilet cistern parts | €50 – €110 |
| Fix leaking pipe under sink | €40 – €90 |
| Adjust water pressure / taps | €30 – €80 |
| Install bidet / attach bidet spray | €50 – €120 |
| Replace flexible hoses | €30 – €70 |
| Install shower enclosure | €80 – €200 |
| Repair clogged shower | €50 – €120 |
| Replace bathroom fan | €40 – €100 |

**2. Furniture Assembly / Interior Install**

| Task | Typical Price |
| --- | --- |
| Assemble IKEA wardrobe | €50 – €120 |
| Assemble IKEA bed | €50 – €120 |
| Assemble IKEA desk | €40 – €100 |
| Assemble IKEA kitchen cabinets | €80 – €200 |
| Assemble flat-pack shelving | €40 – €90 |
| Assemble dining table | €50 – €120 |
| Assemble chairs (per chair) | €15 – €40 |
| Install furniture fittings (handles, hinges) | €30 – €80 |
| Mount coat hooks | €20 – €50 |
| Assemble TV stand | €40 – €90 |

| Install sliding doors | €60 – €150 |
| Assemble modular shelving units | €50 – €120 |
| Install modular closet drawers | €40 – €100 |
| Assemble small bedside tables | €20 – €60 |
| Assemble garden storage units | €50 – €120 |
| Install flat-pack kitchen island | €80 – €200 |
| Assemble modular sofa | €70 – €160 |
| Assemble bunk bed | €60 – €140 |

| Assemble shoe rack / storage bench | €30 – €80 |
| Mount wall-mounted cabinets | €50 – €120 |
| Install small cabinet handles | €20 – €50 |
| Assemble office storage cabinets | €50 – €120 |
| Assemble bookshelf / library units | €50 – €120 |
| Assemble bathroom cabinet | €40 – €90 |
| Install folding wall bed / Murphy bed | €120 – €250 |
| Install custom shelving | €70 – €160 |

**3. Electrical / Tech**

| Task | Typical Price |
| --- | --- |

**What affects the price?**

Several things influence what you'll pay: the size of the job, your location (e.g. city vs rural), how long it takes, whether the helper needs to bring tools or materials, and how urgent it is. Getting a few quotes helps you see a fair range.

**The easier way to get a price**

Instead of calling multiple handymen for quotes, you can simply post your task on Taskorilla. Local helpers will respond and you can choose the person that suits you best.

Post your task now at taskorilla.com and let helpers come to you.
